compatibleScales.musicalContext: Contains all four chord tones. The major 6th (B) is essential for ii-V-I. The standard jazz iim7 sound.
compatibleScales.commonUse: Essential for Dm7-G7-Cmaj7. The definitive iim7 scale in jazz, R&B, neo-soul, and contemporary music. Non-negotiable for jazz harmony.
compatibleScales.famousExamples: The B (13th) is crucial for Dorian color. Smooth voice leading to G7. This IS the ii-V-I sound in jazz.

compatibleScales.musicalContext: Thinking in C major reinforces key center. All notes work since Dm7 is diatonic to C. Simple and effective.
compatibleScales.commonUse: Use for key center emphasis. Great for teaching and maintaining tonal awareness. Works universally in all contexts.
compatibleScales.famousExamples: Think C major, emphasize D as root. Maintains tonal clarity. Perfect for understanding diatonic relationships.

compatibleScales.musicalContext: Contains all four chord tones. The natural minor sound is perfect for vi minor 7. Most common vim7 choice.
compatibleScales.commonUse: Essential for vim7 in C major. Perfect for C-G-Am7-F progressions. Works in pop, rock, folk, and all styles.
compatibleScales.famousExamples: All notes work beautifully. This is the standard vim7 sound. Perfect for emotional, melancholic sections.

compatibleScales.relationship: vi scale - relative minor, shares all notes with B major
compatibleScales.musicalContext: G# minor is the relative minor of B major; they share all seven notes. Provides the minor color within the B major key system
compatibleScales.commonUse: Use when moving to vi chords in B major. Perfect for creating emotional contrast. Works for full minor sections within primarily major songs
compatibleScales.famousExamples: Use when moving to vi chords in B major. Perfect for creating emotional contrast. Works for full minor sections within primarily major songs

compatibleScales.relationship: Natural iii mode with characteristic b2
compatibleScales.musicalContext: All four chord tones present. The b2 (F) creates exotic Phrygian character for iiim7 in major keys.
compatibleScales.commonUse: Perfect for exotic iiim7 color. Use in progressive rock, metal, Spanish music. Less common but highly effective for drama.
compatibleScales.famousExamples: The b9 (F) creates characteristic tension. Use for dark, dramatic, or Spanish passages. Creates exotic color in C major.

compatibleScales.musicalContext: Removes complexity while maintaining minor character. Safe but lacks Dorian sophistication.
compatibleScales.commonUse: Perfect for simple pop/rock approaches. Great for beginners. Use when you want uncomplicated minor sound.
compatibleScales.famousExamples: Very safe but simple. Won't clash but lacks the hip Dorian sound. Good for pop and rock contexts.

compatibleScales.relationship: Relative minor pentatonic - vi relationship to F major
compatibleScales.musicalContext: D minor pentatonic shares many notes with F major pentatonic and works beautifully over F major progressions, adding minor color
compatibleScales.commonUse: Use to add minor flavor over F major progressions. Perfect for creating tension/release in major key songs
compatibleScales.famousExamples: Use to add minor flavor over F major progressions. Perfect for creating tension/release in major key songs
